tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes baseline questionnaire (Q1, 1993-1996) data among all MEC participants: demographics (including age, ethnicity, education, anthropometrics), smoking, medical history, screening history (mammogram, pap smear), family history of cancers, medication use, physical activity, reproductive history, dietary intake (including HEI2010 score).

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix1
variables.description: Dietary patterns: HEI2010 component, total vegetables

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix11
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, refined grains

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix12
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, empty calories

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix2
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, greens and beans

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix3
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, total fruit

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix4
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, whole fruit

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix5
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, whole grains

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix7
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, total protein foods

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix8
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, seafood and plant protein

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_heix9
variables.description: Dietary Patterns, HEI2010 component, fatty acid ratio

variables.name: Q1_DP_HEI2010_totscore
variables.description: Dietary Patterns Healthy Eating Index-2010 score

variables.name: Q1_HATTACK
variables.description: History of Heart Attack or Angina

variables.name: Q1_MULTI1
variables.description: Multivitamin use, with or without minerals (last year, at least once per week)

variables.name: Q1_PAINMED1
variables.description: Other pain medication (other than aspirin/acetaminophen) use

variables.name: Q1_PAINMED2
variables.description: Other pain medication duration of use

variables.name: Q1_W_menostag
variables.description: Menopausal status (expanded); Ages at natural menopause, oophorectomy, other surgery

variables.name: Q1_fh_endo
variables.description: Family history of endometrial cancer in first degree relative (Write-in on QX)

variables.name: Q1_fh_kidn
variables.description: Family history of kidney cancer in first degree relative (Write-in on QX)

variables.name: Q1_fh_panc
variables.description: Family history of pancreas cancer in first degree relative (Write-in on QX)

variables.name: Q1_fh_stom
variables.description: Family history of stomach cancer in 1st degree relative (Write-in on QX)

variables.name: Q1_fh_urntrt
variables.description: Family history of urinary tract cancer in first degree relative (Write-in on QX)

variables.name: Q1_p_monofat
variables.description: Percent of Energy from Monounsaturated Fat

variables.name: Q1_p_polyfat
variables.description: Percent of Energy from Polyunsaturated Fat

variables.name: Q1_p_satfat
variables.description: Percent of Energy from Saturated Fat

variables.name: Q1_w_everbcp
variables.description: Oral contraceptive (birth control pills) use 1 month or longer

variables.name: Q1_w_yrsbcp
variables.description: Duration of  birth control pill use

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes cancer incidence (CA, case reporting through 2015) among MEC participants diagnosed with any incident invasive cancer: age at diagnosis, cancer type, cancer subtype for select types (including breast, colorectum, lung, Non-Hodgkin's lymphoma).

variables.name: CA_ANY_AGEGRP
variables.description: Age at diagnosis of cancer at any site, groups

variables.name: CA_ANY_TYPE
variables.description: Text field containing cancer site groups.

variables.name: CA_ANY_YN
variables.description: First incident cancer at any site after cohort entry

variables.name: CA_BLAD_YN
variables.description: Incident bladder cancer, including in-situ and invasive, after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_BREAST_YN
variables.description: Incident breast cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_COLOREC_YN
variables.description: Incident colorectal cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_ENDO_YN
variables.description: Incident endometrial cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_KIDN_YN
variables.description: Incident kidney cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_LUNG_YN
variables.description: Incident lung cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_NHL_YN
variables.description: Incident NHL after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_PANC_YN
variables.description: Incident pancreatic cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_PROST_YN
variables.description: Incident prostate cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

variables.name: CA_STOM_YN
variables.description: Incident stomach cancer after cohort entry (not necessarily the first incident cancer)

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes biomarker values (BMK) among MEC participants with measurements: age at blood draw, values for alpha tocopherol, C-reactive protein, glucose, insulin, cholesterol, and carotenoids.
This pedigree data table contains family relationships in the format of family ID, subject ID, father ID, mother ID, sex, and monozygotic twin ID.

variables.name: MZ_TWIN_ID
variables.description: Twin ID for monozygotic twins and multiples. An MZ_TWIN_ID is not provided for dizygotic twins or multiples.

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes first follow-up questionnaire (Q2, 1998-2002) data among MEC participants who completed Q2: age, screening history (colonoscopy, gastroscopy, PSA test) not asked on baseline questionnaire.
This sample attributes data table includes body site, histological type, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: HISTOLOGICAL_TYPE
variables.description: Cell or tissue type or subtype of sample

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table includes vital status (reporting through 2015) among all MEC participants: vital status, age at death/end of follow-up, and cause of death.

variables.name: age_exit
variables.description: Age at death / end of follow-up

variables.name: vital
variables.description: Vital Status at end of follow-up

tables.description: The subject consent dataset contains subject IDs, consent group information, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC/MEC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
